sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The container module provides the data structures for holding and managing the various objects created during the analysis, such as events, graphs, and selections. It is designed to handle the data in a structured and efficient way, especially in a multi-threaded environment.

sect1.title: The container Class
sect1.para: The container class is the main data holder for a single input file. It stores all the events, graphs, and selections associated with that file.

listitem.para: container(): The constructor.
~container(): The destructor cleans up all the allocated memory, including the meta data, event templates, graph templates, and selection templates.
void add_meta_data(meta* data, std::string fname): Adds the meta data for the file.
entry_t* add_entry(std::string hash): Adds a new entry_t to the container, identified by a hash.
bool add_event_template(event_template* ev, std::string _label): Adds an event template to the container.
bool add_graph_template(graph_template* gr, std::string _label): Adds a graph template to the container.
bool add_selection_template(selection_template* sel): Adds a selection template to the container.
void get_events(std::vector<event_template*>* out, std::string label): Retrieves all the events with a specific label.
meta* get_meta_data(): Returns the meta data associated with the container.

void compile(size_t* l, int threadIdx): This is the core processing method of the container. It iterates through all the entries and:
Compiles the events.
Compiles and merges the selections, and writes them to output files if specified.
Compiles the graphs and exports them to graph_t objects.
Cleans up the memory used by the templates.
void fill_selections(std::map<std::string, selection_template*>* inpt): Merges the results of the selections from this container into a global map of selections.
void populate_dataloader(dataloader* dl): Populates a dataloader with the graph_t objects created during the compile step.

sect1.title: The entry_t Struct
sect1.para: The entry_t struct is a simple data holder for a single event hash. It stores the event, graph, and selection templates that are associated with that hash.

listitem.para: void init(): Initializes the vectors for storing the templates.
void destroy(): Deletes all the templates stored in the entry.
bool has_event(event_template* ev): Checks if an event template with the same name and tree already exists. If not, it adds the template to the entry.
bool has_graph(graph_template* gr): Checks for and adds a graph template.
bool has_selection(selection_template* sel): Checks for and adds a selection template.
std::string hash: The hash of the event.
std::vector<event_template*> m_event: A vector of event templates.
std::vector<graph_template*> m_graph: A vector of graph templates.
std::vector<selection_template*> m_selection: A vector of selection templates.
std::vector<graph_t*> m_data: A vector of graph_t objects created from the graph templates.
